articles.title: Rumored Patriots target Alec Pierce reportedly expected to make at least $27M per year, might not hit free agency
articles.description:
The Colts opted not to place the franchise tag on Pierce this past week, opening up the possibility he could leave in free agency.

The post Rumored Patriots target Alec Pierce reportedly expected to make at least $27M per year, might not hit free agency appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Colts did not franchise tag Alec Pierce, increasing free agency speculation.
Pierce is projected to earn at least $27M per year if he enters free agency.
Potential signing by the Patriots could impact their receiving corps and local fan interest.

articles.title: ‘Reading felt impossible’: Why she’s running the Boston Marathon for BPL
articles.description:
"Libraries were never just buildings to me. They were sanctuaries that supported and encouraged me."

The post ‘Reading felt impossible’: Why she’s running the Boston Marathon for BPL appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Marguerite Flynn is running the Boston Marathon to support the Boston Public Library.
She views libraries as vital sanctuaries for community support and education.
Flynn's efforts aim to raise awareness about the importance of public libraries in Boston.

articles.title: She’s running the 2026 Boston Marathon for her patient
articles.description:
"Running Boston for her is truly a privilege and we are both honored to support the Ellie Fund."

The post She’s running the 2026 Boston Marathon for her patient appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Amy Comander will run the 2026 Boston Marathon to support her patient.
Comander is raising funds for the Ellie Fund, aiding breast cancer patients.
The marathon participation highlights local support for cancer awareness in Boston.

articles.title: Masataka Yoshida’s late HR lifts Japan over Australia to take first place in group at World Baseball Classic
articles.description:
Yoshida hit a two-run homer in the seventh inning to help Japan beat Australia, 4-3.

The post Masataka Yoshida’s late HR lifts Japan over Australia to take first place in group at World Baseball Classic appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Masataka Yoshida hit a two-run homer in the seventh inning.
Japan defeated Australia 4-3, securing first place in their group.
Yoshida's performance boosts local interest in the Red Sox ahead of the season.
